documentation:
# bpy-helper

A lightweight alternative to BlenderProc

[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/bpy-helper.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/bpy-helper/) [![Upload Python Package](https://github.com/iamNCJ/bpy-helper/actions/workflows/python-publish.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/iamNCJ/bpy-helper/actions/workflows/python-publish.yml)

## Why bpy-helper instead of BlenderProc?

BlenderProc needs a separate blender installation, and a lot of restrictions when using with other pypi packages. Debugging a BlenderProc script is also painful especially when you are using a headless environment. Hence I decide to build my own blender wrapper using `bpy`.

## Installation

```bash
pip install bpy-helper
```

## Projects using bpy-helper

- [NRHints: Relighting Neural Radiance Fields with Shadow and Highlight Hints](https://nrhints.github.io/): bpy-helper is derived from the data rendering scripts of NRHints.
- [DiLightNet: Fine-grained Lighting Control for Diffusion-based Image Generation](https://dilightnet.github.io/): bpy-helper is used to render the training data and radiance hints for DiLightNet.
- [GS^3: Efficient Relighting with Triple Gaussian Splatting](https://gsrelight.github.io/): bpy-helper is used to render the training data for GS^3.

functions.name: bpy_helper.utils.stdout_redirected
documentation:
A context manager to temporarily redirect stdout to another file. This is useful when you want to suppress the
output from blender, especially when you are rendering a large number of images.

Example usage:
\>>> import os
\>>> filename = os.devnull
\>>> with stdout_redirected(to=filename):
... print("from Python")
... os.system("echo non-Python applications are also supported")

```
param to

The file to redirect stdout to. Default is os.devnull.

return

The context manager.
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.random.gen_random_pts_around_origin
documentation:
Generate random points around the origin

```
param seed

random seed

param N

number of points

param min_dist_to_origin

minimum distance to the origin

param max_dist_to_origin

maximum distance to the origin

param min_theta_in_degree

minimum theta in degree

param max_theta_in_degree

maximum theta in degree

param z_up

if True, z is up, otherwise y is up

return

list of point positions
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.scene.remove_default_objects
documentation: Removes the default objects from the scene.

functions.name: bpy_helper.scene.reset_scene
documentation: Resets the scene to the default state.

functions.name: bpy_helper.scene.import_3d_model
documentation: Loads a 3d model into the scene.

functions.name: bpy_helper.scene.scene_root_objects
documentation: Yields all root objects in the scene.

functions.name: bpy_helper.scene.scene_meshes
documentation: Yields all mesh objects in the scene.

functions.name: bpy_helper.scene.scene_bbox
documentation:
Compute the bounding box of the scene.

```
param single_obj

if not None, only compute the bounding box for this object

param ignore_matrix

if True, ignore the transformation matrix of the object

return

bounding box min and max
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.scene.get_center
documentation: Compute the center value of a list of numbers.

functions.name: bpy_helper.scene.scene_sphere
documentation:
Compute the bounding sphere of the scene.

```
param single_obj

if not None, only compute the bounding sphere for this object

param ignore_matrix

if True, ignore the transformation matrix of the object

return

bounding sphere center and radius
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.scene.normalize_scene
documentation:
Normalize the scene by scaling and translating all objects.

```
param scale

scale factor

param offset

translation offset

param use_bounding_sphere

if True, use the bounding sphere to compute the scale factor

param target_scale

the target scale factor

return

scale factor and translation offset
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.scene.scale_from_trimesh
documentation:
Compute the scale factor from a trimesh file. Some GLB models contains animation data, which can effect the scale
factor. This function uses trimesh to compute the scale factor, which can avoid the animation data.

```
param filename

the file name of the 3D object

param use_bounding_sphere

if True, use the bounding sphere to compute the scale factor
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.io.save_blend_file
documentation:
Save the current blend file

```
param filepath

file path to save
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.io.get_nodes_created_in_func
documentation:
Returns all nodes which are created in the given function

```
param nodes

list of nodes of the current material

param created_in_func

return all nodes created in the given function

return

The list of nodes with the given type.
```
adapted from BlenderProc

functions.name: bpy_helper.io.get_nodes_with_type
documentation:
Returns all nodes which are of the given node_type

```
param nodes

list of nodes of the current material

param node_type

node types

param created_in_func

Only return nodes created by the specified function

return

list of nodes, which belong to the type
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.io.get_the_one_node_with_type
documentation:
Returns the one node which is of the given node_type

This function will only work if there is only one of the nodes of this type.

```
param nodes

list of nodes of the current material

param node_type

node types

param created_in_func

only return node created by the specified function

return

node of the node type
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.io.render_depth_map
documentation:
Render depth map

```
param output_dir

output directory

param file_prefix

file prefix, default is 'depth'
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.io.render_normal_map
documentation:
Render normal map

```
param output_dir

output directory

param file_prefix

file prefix, default is 'normal'
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.geometry.set_smooth_shading
documentation: Sets smooth shading for all objects in the scene.

functions.name: bpy_helper.light.remove_all_lights
documentation: Remove all lights in the scene

functions.name: bpy_helper.light.create_point_light
documentation:
Creates a point light at the given location.

```
param location

The location of the light

param power

The power of the light, default is 800

param rgb

The color of the light, default is white

param hard_shadow

If true, the shadow will be hard. Default is False

param keep_other_lights

If true, the other lights will not be removed. Default is False

return

The light object
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.light.create_area_light
documentation:
Creates an area light at the given location.

```
param location

The location of the light

param power

The power of the light, default is 800

param size

The size of the light, default is 5

param keep_other_lights

If true, the other lights will not be removed. Default is False

return

The light object
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.light.create_directional_light
documentation:
Creates a directional light at the given location.

```
param location

The location of the light

param power

The power of the light, default is 800

param rgb

The color of the light, default is white

param keep_other_lights

If true, the other lights will not be removed. Default is False

return

The light object
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.light.set_env_light
documentation:
Sets the world background to the given hdr_file.

```
param path_to_hdr_file

Path to the .exr file

param strength

The brightness of the background.

param rotation_euler

The euler angles of the background.

param keep_other_lights

If true, the other lights will not be removed.
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.camera.remove_all_cameras
documentation: Remove all cameras in the scene

functions.name: bpy_helper.camera.create_camera
documentation:
Create camera in the scene

```
param transform_matrix

camera to world matrix

param fov

field of view in degrees, default is 30

return

camera object
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.camera.look_at_to_c2w
documentation:
Look at transform matrix

```
param camera_position

camera position

param target_position

target position, default is origin

param up_dir

up vector, default is z-axis up

return

camera to world matrix
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.material.clear_emission_and_alpha_nodes
documentation: Clear all emission and alpha nodes in the scene, including all objects' materials

functions.name: bpy_helper.material.override_normal_map_op
documentation: Override normal map for the material (to attach a precomputed world-space normal map)

functions.name: bpy_helper.material.create_white_diffuse_material
documentation:
Create white diffuse material

```
param override_normal_map

override normal map

param normal_map_path

normal map path

param material_name

material name, default is "White_Diffuse_Material"

return

white diffuse material
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.material.create_white_emmissive_material
documentation:
Create white emissive material

```
param strength

emission strength

param override_normal_map

override normal map

param normal_map_path

normal map path

param material_name

material name, default is "White_Emissive_Material"

return

white emissive material
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.material.create_specular_ggx_material
documentation:
Create specular GGX material

```
param r

roughness, default is 0.34

param override_normal_map

whether to override normal map, default is False

param normal_map_path

normal map path, if override_normal_map is True

param material_name

material name, default is "Specular_GGX_Material"

return

specular GGX material
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.material.create_invisible_material
documentation:
Create invisible material

```
param material_name

material name, default is "Invisible_Material"

return

invisible material
```

functions.name: bpy_helper.material.create_schlick_shader
documentation: Create Schlick shader node group for the specular reflection model

functions.name: bpy_helper.material.create_specular_roughness_bsdf
documentation: Create specular-roughness workflow BSDF Node Group

functions.name: bpy_helper.material.create_specular_roughness_material
documentation:
Create specular-roughness material

```
param diffuse_color

diffuse color, default is (1.0, 1.0, 1.0)

param specular_color

specular color, default is (0.0, 0.0, 0.0)

param roughness

roughness, default is 1.0

param material_name

material name, default is "Specular_Roughness_Material"

return

specular-roughness material
```
